title: Sobbing Teyana Taylor Sends Message to 'Little Brown Girls' After Golden Globes Win
text: The 35-year-old actress triumphed in the Best Supporting Female Actor category at the 2026 Golden Globes ceremony held January 11 at the Beverly Hilton. She secured the award for her role in One Battle After Another, directed by Paul Thomas Anderson, surpassing nominees including Ariana Grande, Amy Madigan, Emily Blunt, Elle Fanning, and Inga Ibsdotter Lilleaas. During her acceptance speech, an emotional Taylor shouted out to her children watching upstairs, then delivered an inspiring message to her community. She stated: Our softness is not a liability. Our light does not need permission to shine. We belong in every room. Taylor had previously emphasized the significance of her role's exploration of postpartum depression, describing it as an underrepresented topic. Her character, Perfidia Beverly Hills, makes controversial choices, yet Taylor highlighted the character's refusal to diminish herself, noting how women are frequently pressured to suppress their confidence and ambition.
